This is the **Gardner–Collins toggle switch** (Nature, 2000), a foundational circuit in therapeutic synthetic biology. --- ### 3.4 Analytical Matrix — Comparing Therapeutic Modalities | Modality | Cargo / Mechanism | Delivery Vehicle | Key Control Element | KTU-Mentioned Example | |---|---|---|---|---| | CAR-T | Chimeric antigen receptor | Ex-vivo lentiviral transduction | synNotch / AND-gate | Tisagenlecleucel (Kymriah®) | | TCR-T | Engineered T-cell receptor | Lentivirus | HLA-restricted targeting | NeoTCR-P1 | | Gene addition | SMN1, RPE65, Factor IX | AAV (AAV9, AAV2) | Tissue-specific promoter | Zolgensma®, Luxturna® | | Gene editing | Cas9, base editor, prime editor | LNP, electroporation | sgRNA specificity | CASGEVY® (ex vivo) | | Oncolytic virus | GM-CSF, TGF-β trap | Herpes simplex, measles | Tumor-selective promoter | T-VEC (Imlygic®) | | Engineered probiotic | Phenylalanine ammonia lyase, GLP-1 | Oral *E. coli* Nissle 1917 | pH-inducible promoter | SYNB1618 (PKU), SYNB8802 | | mRNA vaccine | Spike protein, tumor neoantigen | Lipid nanoparticle (LNP) | Modified nucleosides | BNT162b2, mRNA-4157 | | Phage therapy | Lytic enzymes, CRISPR-payloads | T4, T7 phages | Engineered host range | SNIPR001, Locus LN-145 | | siRNA | RISC-mediated mRNA cleavage | GalNAc conjugate, LNP | Chemical modifications | Givosiran, Patisiran | --- ## 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics ### 4.1 Block Diagram — Generic Therapeutic Synthetic Gene Circuit ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph INPUT["INPUT LAYER - Sensing"] BM["Biomarker
(hPGK, PSA, Lactate)"] PR["Synthetic Promoter
or Receptor"] end subgraph PROCESS["PROCESSING LAYER - Logic"] G1["AND Gate
Repressor 1"] G2["OR Gate
Repressor 2"] NOT["NOT Gate
Inverter"] end subgraph OUTPUT["OUTPUT LAYER - Effector"] EF1["Therapeutic Protein
(Antibody, Cytokine)"] EF2["Kill Payload
(Granzyme, TRAIL)"] end subgraph SAFETY["SAFETY LAYER - Containment"] KS1["Kill Switch 1
Auxotrophy"] KS2["Kill Switch 2
Thermal Trigger"] end BM --> PR PR --> G1 G1 --> G2 G2 --> NOT NOT --> EF1 NOT --> EF2 EF1 --> KS1 EF2 --> KS2 style INPUT fill:#e1f5ff style PROCESS fill:#fff4cc style OUTPUT fill:#ffe1e1 style SAFETY fill:#d4f4dd ``` ### 4.2 Architecture — CAR-T Dual-Antigen Logic Gate (synNotch AND-Gate) ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph TUMOR["TUMOR CELL"] AG_A["Antigen A
(CD19)"] AG_B["Antigen B
(BCMA)"] end subgraph CART["ENGINEERED CAR-T CELL"] SYN1["synNotch Receptor A
Binds Antigen A"] TF["Synthetic Transcription
Factor Released"] PRO["Inducible Promoter
driven by TF"] CAR2["CAR Receptor B
Binds Antigen B"] KILL["Cytotoxic Effector
Perforin, Granzyme B"] end AG_A -->|bind| SYN1 SYN1 -->|cleavage| TF TF -->|transactivate| PRO PRO -->|express| CAR2 AG_B -->|bind| CAR2 CAR2 -->|activate| KILL style TUMOR fill:#ffe1e1 style CART fill:#e1f5ff ``` > **Reading the diagram:** Antigen A *primes* the CAR-T by expressing the CAR (via the synNotch → TF → inducible promoter cascade). Antigen B *activates* the now-present CAR. The cell kills **only** when **both** antigens are present — a true AND-gate that protects healthy tissue expressing only one antigen. ### 4.3 Block-Level Functional Architecture — Engineered Probiotic Therapy Pipeline ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph ORAL["ORAL ADMINISTRATION"] CAP["Encapsulated
E. coli Nissle 1917"] end subgraph GI["GASTROINTESTINAL TRACT"] RELEASE["pH-Triggered
Release at pH 6.5-7.0"] SENSE["Sensor Module
Detects Disease Marker"] end subgraph ACT["THERAPEUTIC ACTION"] SYN["Enzyme Synthesis
e.g. PAL, GLP-1, NAPE"] SEC["Secretion via
Type III Secretion System"] end subgraph END["CLINICAL OUTCOME"] MET["Metabolite
Degradation"] EFF["Therapeutic
Effect"] end subgraph EXIT["EXCRETION + KILL SWITCH"] KS["Cold-Shock
Inducible kill"] OUT["Safe Expulsion

KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap ### 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each) > **[KTU University Exam — May 2024, Model Question 1]** > **Q1.** Define *therapeutic synthetic biology* and list **two** clinical products based on this discipline. **Model Answer:** Therapeutic synthetic biology is the rational design and construction of novel biological components, genetic circuits, and whole-cell systems for the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of human disease. It applies engineering principles (modularity, abstraction, standardization) to build programmable therapeutics such as CAR-T cells, mRNA vaccines, engineered probiotics, and oncolytic viruses. Two clinical products: - **Kymriah® (tisagenlecleucel)** — autologous CAR-T cell therapy for B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. - **Comirnaty® (BNT162b2)** — synthetic mRNA vaccine encoding SARS-CoV-2 spike protein delivered via lipid nanoparticles. (3 marks: 1.5 for definition, 1.5 for examples with brief mechanism.) --- > **[KTU University Exam — Dec 2023, Model Question 2]** > **Q2.** What is a *kill switch* in therapeutic synthetic biology? Mention **two** types with one-line descriptions. **Model Answer:** A kill switch is a genetic safety device engineered into a therapeutic cell to ensure its controlled elimination after fulfilling its function, thereby preventing uncontrolled proliferation or environmental release. Two types: - **Auxotrophic kill switch:** The therapeutic cell is engineered to lack an essential biosynthetic gene (e.g., *thyA* or *asd*); removal of the supplemented metabolite (thymidine, DAP) causes cell death. - **Cold-shock–inducible kill switch:** A toxin gene (e.g., *ccdB* or *holin*) is placed under a cold-shock promoter, so a drop in temperature triggers toxin expression and cell lysis. (3 marks.) --- ### 5.2 Part B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks Each) > **[KTU University Exam — Model Paper 2024, Module 4 Internal Choice]** > > **Question A (14 marks):** > **(a)** With a neat labeled diagram, describe the architecture and working of a **logic-gated CAR-T cell** for cancer immunotherapy. Explain how an AND-gate reduces on-target off-tumor toxicity. (7 marks) > **(b)** Derive the **Michaelis–Menten equation** for an engineered cytosine deaminase enzyme used in suicide gene therapy. Discuss how a lower $K_m$ variant improves the therapeutic index. (7 marks) > > **OR** > > **Question B (14 marks):** > **(a)** Discuss **mRNA vaccine design** as a therapeutic application of synthetic biology, covering cap structure, modified nucleosides, and lipid nanoparticle delivery. (7 marks) > **(b)** Explain the **toggle switch bistability** in a therapeutic memory circuit. State the steady-state equations and the bistability condition. (7 marks) --- ### 5.3 Model Solutions #### **Question A — Part (a) Solution** A logic-gated CAR-T cell uses **two synthetic receptors**: - **Receptor 1 (synNotch):** A synthetic Notch receptor that binds Antigen A and, upon binding, releases a synthetic transcription factor (TF) via intramembrane proteolysis. - **Receptor 2 (CAR):** A chimeric antigen receptor that binds Antigen B and triggers T-cell activation (perforin / granzyme secretion). **Working:** 1. Antigen A binds the synNotch → TF is released → TF transactivates an inducible promoter → CAR gene is transcribed. 2. Antigen B binds the newly expressed CAR → T-cell activation → tumor lysis. **AND-gate logic:** T-cell killing occurs **only** if **both** antigens A and B are present on the target cell. Tumors expressing Antigen A alone or Antigen B alone are spared. **Reduction of on-target off-tumor toxicity:** Healthy tissues that express one antigen (e.g., low levels of CD19 in normal B-cell precursors) but not the second antigen will **not** trigger killing, dramatically narrowing the side-effect profile. (7 marks: 2 diagram, 3 mechanism, 2 clinical rationale.) #### **Question A — Part (b) Solution** The derivation is as shown in **Section 3.1** of these notes. Key points for full marks: **Step 1:** Write the reaction scheme with rate constants. **Step 2:** Apply the quasi-steady-state assumption on [ES]. **Step 3:** Derive $K_m = (k_{-1} + k_2)/k_1$. **Step 4:** Use enzyme conservation $[E]_T = [E] + [ES]$. **Step 5:** Arrive at $v = V_{\max}[S] / (K_m + [S])$. **Lower $K_m$ → higher therapeutic index:** A lower $K_m$ means the prodrug 5-FC is converted to cytotoxic 5-FU more efficiently at low substrate concentrations. Hence a smaller enzyme dose is required to achieve the tumor-killing 5-FU concentration, reducing systemic exposure and raising the therapeutic index. (7 marks: 5 derivation, 2 discussion.) --- #### **Question B — Part (a) Solution** mRNA vaccine design encompasses: - **5' Cap structure:** A **Cap-1** structure (m⁷GpppNm) is added co-transcriptionally to prevent innate immune detection by RIG-I / MDA5 and to enable ribosomal recognition via eIF4E. - **Modified nucleosides:** Replacement of uridine with **N1-methylpseudouridine (m¹Ψ)** reduces TLR7/8 activation, increases translational efficiency, and improves mRNA stability. - **Poly(A) tail:** A 100–150 nt poly(A) tail enhances translation and mRNA half-life. - **LNP delivery:** The mRNA is encapsulated in a lipid nanoparticle composed of an ionizable lipid (e.g., SM-102, ALC-0315), a helper lipid (DSPC), cholesterol, and PEG-lipid. LNPs protect the mRNA, enable cellular uptake via apolipoprotein E / LDLR, and allow endosomal escape. - **Therapeutic impact:** mRNA vaccines against SARS-CoV-2 (BNT162b2, mRNA-1273) and personalized cancer neoantigens (mRNA-4157/V940) demonstrate the platform's speed, scalability, and clinical efficacy. (7 marks.) #### **Question B — Part (b) Solution** The **Gardner–Collins toggle switch** consists of two mutually repressing genes (LacI represses *tetR*, TetR represses *lacI*). **Steady-state equations:** $$ \frac{dx}{dt} = \frac{\alpha_1}{1 + (y/K_1)^{n}} - \gamma_1 x = 0 $$ $$ \frac{dy}{dt} = \frac{\alpha_2}{1 + (x/K_2)^{m}} - \gamma_2 y = 0 $$ Solving yields the *x-nullcline* and *y-nullcline*. The system exhibits **two stable steady states** and **one unstable saddle point** when the product of repression strengths exceeds a critical threshold: $$ \frac{\alpha_1 \alpha_2}{\gamma_1 \gamma_2} > K_1 K_2 \cdot \kappa $$ **Therapeutic memory:** Once flipped into the "ON" state by a transient biomarker (e.g., TNF-α), the therapeutic cell stably expresses the drug (e.g., anti–IL-6 antibody) for the duration of the cell's lifetime, providing sustained relief in chronic inflammation. (7 marks: 3 diagram description, 2 steady-state derivation, 2 bistability condition + therapeutic significance.) --- ### 5.4 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout > [!WARNING] > **Common Mark-Loss Traps in Module 4 Answers:** > 1. **Definition completeness:** Writing *"synthetic biology is genetic engineering"* loses 1 mark. The KTU key requires the words *rational design*, *engineered components*, and *novel function*. > 2. **Equation omission:** The Michaelis–Menten derivation demands **explicit** QSSA, enzyme conservation, and final boxed form. Skipping any one step forfeits up to 2 marks. > 3. **Diagram labeling:** A block diagram with unlabeled arrows scores zero. Always name the *biomarker*, *promoter*, *effector*, and *kill switch*. > 4. **Numerical units:** A dose-response answer with no units on TI / IC50 / PFU loses 0.5–1 mark. > 5. **Confusing CAR-T and TCR-T:** CAR-T is *HLA-independent* (recognizes surface antigen natively); TCR-T is *HLA-restricted* (recognizes peptide–MHC). Mixing them up costs 1 mark. > 6. **Toggle switch nullclines:** Many students write the equations but forget to set $dx/dt = dy/dt = 0$. Always show the steady-state step explicitly. --- ### 5.5 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember > [!IMPORTANT] > **High-Density Revision Checklist — Module 4: Therapeutic Applications of Synthetic Biology** **Core Concepts (must know verbatim):** - Therapeutic synthetic biology = rational engineering of biological systems for disease treatment. - Five pillars: **CAR-T, gene therapy, engineered probiotics, nucleic acid therapeutics, oncolytics/phages**. - Logic gates (AND, OR, NOT) reduce off-target effects in cell therapy. **Quantitative Must-Knows:** - **Therapeutic Index:** $\text{TI} = \text{TD}_{50} / \text{ED}_{50}$. - **Michaelis–Menten:** $v = V_{\max} [S] / (K_m + [S])$ — central to GDEPT/suicide gene therapy. - **Hill dose-response with IC50** — used for antibody and inhibitor dose curves. - **Toggle switch bistability condition** — product of repression strengths > threshold. - **MOI = PFU / Cells** — for oncolytic virus and phage dosing. **Engineering Principles:** - **Modularity:** Sensor + Processor + Effector + Safety. - **Abstraction:** DNA → Part → Device → System. - **Standardization:** Use of BioBrick / SEVA / MoClo standards for therapeutic parts. - **Orthogonality:** Therapeutic circuits must not cross-talk with host genome. **Clinical Milestones (Frequently Asked in 2-mark questions):** - **Kymriah® (2017)** — first FDA-approved CAR-T. - **Luxturna® (2017)** — first in-vivo gene therapy (AAV2-RPE65). - **Zolgensma® (2019)** — AAV9-SMN1 for SMA. - **Comirnaty® & Spikevax® (2020)** — first mRNA vaccines. - **CASGEVY® (2023)** — first CRISPR-Cas9–based therapy (ex vivo). **Safety & Ethics (5–10 mark questions):** - **Kill switches** (auxotrophy, cold-shock, antibiotic-controlled). - **Containment levels** (BSL-1, BSL-2 for engineered probiotics). - **Germline vs. somatic editing** — germline editing is currently restricted. - **Informed consent** and equitable access to gene therapies. **Diagram Essentials for Full Marks:** - Always label the biomarker input, the logic gate, the effector output, and the kill switch. - Use arrows with verb labels (e.g., "binds," "transactivates," "cleaves"). - Color-code the four layers (sensing, processing, actuation, safety) for clarity. **Key Formulas Quick-Fire:** - $v = \dfrac{V_{\max} [S]}{K_m + [S]}$ - $\text{TI} = \text{TD}_{50} / \text{ED}_{50}$ - $\theta = [L]^n / (K_d + [L]^n)$ - $\text{MOI} = \text{PFU} / \text{Cells}$ - $k = A e^{-E_a / RT}$ > **Final KTU Tip:** Questions on Module 4 frequently test **clinical translation awareness** — always pair a circuit concept with at least one named FDA-approved product in your answer to earn the higher mark band (80%+).
